PDS Element: Core Functionality: Effective Particle and Debris Removal
Justification: The primary purpose is to effectively remove particulate matter and debris, maintaining performance over the product's lifespan.; Filtration: Utilize HEPA filtration (99.97% of particles down to 0.3 microns), with specific attention to efficiency for PM2.5 particles.; Performance maintenance: Ensure filtration and suction performance are maintained throughout the product's expected lifetime, with clear indicators for filter replacement.; Material separation: Design for easy separation of collected material from the dustbin for potential reuse or recycling.; Suction power: Target >150 Air Watts (AW) measured under standardized conditions (e.g., IEC 60312-1) on the highest power setting.; Airflow rate: Target >30 L/s measured at 20 hPa with standardized accessories.

Minimize environmental impact through reduced energy consumption during operation and throughout the lifecycle.; Motor efficiency: Target motor efficiency of >90% at optimal load.; Standby power: Standby power consumption not to exceed 0.5W.; Operational energy consumption: Achieve Energy Star certification requirements. Target <40 kWh/year based on standardized usage.; Power management: Implement intelligent power management for optimized energy use across different modes.; Manufacturing energy: Consider and aim to reduce energy consumed during manufacturing and transportation processes.

Prioritize materials with minimal environmental impact, enabling a circular economy through recyclability and responsible sourcing.; Recycled content: Mandate a minimum of 50% post-consumer recycled (PCR) content for all plastic and metal components, targeting 75% for structural parts.; Material types: Prioritize mono-materials and easily separable combinations. Minimize the number of different material types used. Avoid composite materials and glues.; Recyclability labeling: All plastic components must be clearly labeled with resin identification codes (e.g., PP, ABS) embossed in the mold.; Design for disassembly: Facilitate easy disassembly for repair and recycling.; Biodegradable materials: Use cautiously for specific, controlled applications (e.g., packaging), avoiding where durability or end-of-life processing is uncertain.

Extend product lifespan to reduce waste and the need for frequent replacements, supported by rigorous testing and modular design.; Product lifespan: Target 10 years of typical use or 2000 operational hours, validated through accelerated life testing.; Motor durability: Main motor rated for >5000 operational cycles.; Wear-resistant components: Key components (e.g., brush roll, seals) designed for enhanced wear resistance.; Modular design: Design for easy replacement of key wear parts (filters, brush rolls, battery if applicable).; Spare parts availability: Ensure availability of spare parts for at least 7 years post-production.; Testing protocols: Define rigorous testing for structural integrity, motor durability, and component wear.

PDS Element: User Experience: Ease of Use and Ergonomics
Justification: Ensure comfortable, intuitive, and efficient operation, including simplified maintenance and repair.; Weight: Target total product weight under 5kg, with ergonomic design for comfortable handling.; Maintenance tasks: Simplify dustbin emptying and filter cleaning for hygiene, minimal dust re-emission, and ease of disassembly.; Control interface: Design intuitive controls with minimal buttons (e.g., <3 primary controls).; Disassembly for repair: Ensure ease of disassembly using simple tools.; Handling: Ergonomic design for comfortable grip and maneuverability in various orientations.

Minimize operational noise to enhance user comfort and reduce disturbance.; Maximum noise level: Target a maximum of 70 dB(A) measured at 1 meter on a hard floor.; Noise reduction methods: Achieve target through optimized airflow, motor enclosure, and judicious use of lightweight, acoustically effective dampening materials.; Material impact: Avoid excessive material additions for noise reduction that compromise weight or recyclability.

Design for modularity, easy component replacement, and clear repair guidance.; Modularity: Key components (motor, battery, electronics, brush head) accessible and replaceable with simple tools.; Tool-less maintenance: Specify tool-less disassembly for user-level maintenance (e.g., filter changes).; Repair guides: Provide clear, illustrated repair guides for common issues.; Spare parts: Ensure spare parts are available for at least 7 years.

Ensure superior indoor air quality by effectively capturing fine particles and allergens.; Filtration standard: Utilize HEPA filtration (99.97% of particles down to 0.3 microns).; Filter materials: Specify recyclable or reusable filter materials where feasible.; Disposable filters: If used, must be easily separable from housings for recycling.; Filter lifespan: Define filter lifespan and ease of replacement, ensuring performance is maintained.

Provide adequate capacity for typical cleaning sessions and ensure hygienic, efficient emptying with minimal dust re-emission.; Capacity: For bagless models, target 1.5-2L capacity sized for typical household use.; Emptying: Design for easy, hygienic emptying using a sealed mechanism with minimal dust re-emission.; Material: Dustbins made from durable, easily cleanable, and recyclable plastic.; Bagged option: If bags are used, they must be made from compostable or easily recyclable materials.; Material separation: Consider how collected material can be easily separated for its next life.

Guarantee user safety through standard features and design considerations for repair and recycling.; Standard features: Incorporate automatic overheat protection and secure electrical connections.; Material safety: Ensure electrical components and casings are fire retardant and non-toxic.; Repair/Recycling compatibility: Safety features must not impede disassembly for repair or recycling.; Battery safety (if applicable): Battery must be easily removable and designed for safe handling and recycling.

Minimize ongoing costs through energy efficiency, durability, and affordable, recyclable components.; Operating costs: Prioritize energy efficiency and durability to reduce long-term expenses.; Replacement parts: Ensure replacement parts (filters, brush rolls, batteries) are readily available and competitively priced.; Warranty: Clearly define the warranty period as part of the total cost of ownership.

Adopt a modern, clean, and minimalist design that communicates durability and quality.; Design style: Modern, clean, and minimalist with natural colors achieved through material choice and processing, not coatings that hinder recyclability.; Material perception: Materials should convey durability and quality.; Form: Avoid complex shapes that increase manufacturing difficulty or hinder disassembly and recycling.

Design for disassembly, clear material labeling, and implement a take-back program for maximum material recovery.; Disassembly: Design for disassembly using simple, standardized fasteners (e.g., Torx screws) and avoiding glues.; Material labeling: All components clearly labeled with material type for recycling.; Take-back program: Implement a product take-back program in partnership with recycling facilities.; Material recovery: Aim for maximum material recovery and recycling, with clear pathways for each component.

Minimize environmental impact through material choice, design, and responsible sourcing.; Recycled content: Minimum 80% post-consumer recycled (PCR) content for all packaging materials.; Recyclability/Compostability: All packaging components must be recyclable or compostable.; Right-sizing: Design for 'right-sizing' to minimize void fill and transportation emissions.; Material restrictions: Avoid single-use plastics and styrofoam.; Reusable packaging: Consider reusable packaging for spare parts and returned products.

Provide a versatile cleaning solution with durable, easy-to-use, and recyclable attachments.; Attachments: Design attachments for durability, ease of cleaning, and recyclability.; Attachment system: Robust, easy to use, and cost-effective.; Disassembly: Ensure attachments can be easily disassembled for repair or recycling.; Floor head: Prioritize a single, well-designed floor head with simple adjustments for versatility over multiple specialized heads.
